task.title: Persist the task completion lifecycle
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/423 by adding one bounded terminal completion transition to the persisted task lifecycle without defining completion output, cancellation, retries, sessions, or runtime behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Completing an active task appends task.completed payload version one and reloads the original ID and goal with completed status after reopening SQLite.
Completing a missing or already completed task returns an explicit domain error without creating or changing valid history.
Replay accepts only task.started followed optionally by task.completed and rejects recognized lifecycle events in any other order.
Existing replay compatibility failures remain explicit.
Public behavior and non-goals are documented, pinned verification passes, and the required pull-request gates permit squash auto-merge.

attempts.summary: Translated the next task lifecycle step into a bounded durable issue and wrote completion integration tests before production code.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused RED command failed because TaskStore::complete, TaskStatus::Completed, TaskStoreError::NotFound, and TaskStoreError::AlreadyCompleted did not exist.
attempts.patch: Added tests for completion persistence, missing and repeated completion errors, and invalid completion ordering.

attempts.summary: Implemented a versioned completion event and reconstructed active or completed task state from history.
attempts.diagnostic: All 12 lifecycle integration tests passed, including reopen persistence, explicit domain errors, completion-first rejection, and rejection of recognized events after completion.
attempts.patch: Added TaskStatus::Completed, task.completed payload version one, TaskStore::complete, explicit NotFound and AlreadyCompleted errors, exact-version append protection, and bounded replay shapes.

attempts.summary: Refactored, documented, and independently reviewed the implementation before commit.
attempts.diagnostic: The public contract documented empty completion payloads, exact expected version one, valid history shapes, domain errors, and explicit non-goals. An independent read-only Codex review found no actionable defect.
attempts.patch: Kept completion output-free and synchronous, reused the typed event log for persistence and optimistic concurrency, and made no session or runtime assumptions.

attempts.summary: Ran local and hosted quality gates, monitored automated review, and completed squash auto-merge.
attempts.diagnostic: The pinned just verify gate passed 46 workspace tests plus formatting, checking, Clippy, SecretSpec, and diff checks. GitHub Rust quality run 29374060007 passed. CodeRabbit's required status passed and its review reported one non-blocking nitpick: the append-level concurrent completion race arm is not directly exercised by a two-writer test. Pull request 424 then squash-merged as 80d155ba.
attempts.patch: No blocking review correction was required. The concurrency-test suggestion remained a disclosed follow-up rather than expanding the merged issue after its gates passed.

attempts.summary: Added self-verification evidence to the development record using the complete artifact filename.
attempts.diagnostic: The sanitation validator rejected the verification command because a secret-marker substring appeared across a word boundary in the otherwise harmless artifact filename.
attempts.patch: Kept the evidence but identified the filename text, not a credential, as the source of the deterministic rejection.

attempts.summary: Captured the merged lifecycle episode and review nuance in the Vela-native development corpus.
attempts.diagnostic: Using the numeric record prefix with a wildcard avoided the false-positive marker while still identifying one artifact. The record validator accepted it, corpus inspection reported 25 valid and zero invalid records, full pinned verification passed, and an independent read-only review found no correctness issue.
attempts.patch: Added one sanitized positive record that separates passing gates from the non-blocking concurrency coverage suggestion and preserves task, attempt, diagnostic, fix, rationale, and verification evidence.

outcome.summary: Vela's kernel can now durably complete an active task, reload completed state, reject invalid transitions, and preserve explicit replay failures while leaving later lifecycle semantics undefined.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test -p vela-kernel --test task_lifecycle # RED: completion API and errors were missing
nix develop --command cargo test -p vela-kernel --test task_lifecycle # GREEN: 12 passed, 0 failed
nix develop --command just verify before pull request 424 # 46 workspace tests and all local gates passed
Independent read-only Codex pre-commit review of issue 423 changes: no actionable defects
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29374060007 for pull request 424
Required CodeRabbit status and semantic review for pull request 424
CodeRabbit review nitpick: append-level concurrent completion race branch lacks a direct two-writer integration test
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0025*.json # result: valid development record
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 25 valid, 0 invalid
nix develop --command just verify with development record 0025
Independent read-only review of issue 425 corpus change: no actionable correctness issue

lessons: A terminal lifecycle state can remain small: an empty completion event is sufficient when completion output and later transitions are explicit non-goals.
Replaying only named valid event shapes rejects completion-first, duplicate, and post-terminal histories without silently repairing persisted data.
Loading before completion provides domain-specific missing and already-completed errors, while an exact-version append protects the transition from racing writers.
A successful sequential duplicate test does not exercise the append-level optimistic-concurrency error arm; direct two-writer coverage is a distinct follow-up concern.
Automated review findings should retain their severity: a disclosed non-blocking test nitpick is evidence for future prioritization, not a reason to claim a blocking production defect.
Explicit completion non-goals prevent a persisted state transition from accidentally defining sessions, retries, output, model interaction, or runtime architecture.
